findings.summary: Framed absence of features as presence of problems. 10 'gaps' listed, 0 have incident history. Built 7-layer plan for 1 real problem.
findings.evidence: git log + selve search found 0 incidents for 6/7 proposed layers
findings.proposed_fix: Pain-point gate: git log + session logs + selve before planning. No incident = DROP.

findings.summary: Model review prompt presupposed architecture should exist. Asked 'what is wrong with this?' not 'should this exist?' Both models critiqued within frame. Re-prompt with incidents: Gemini said DROP 6/7.
findings.evidence: First review: 100 claims, 0 questioned necessity. Re-prompt: DROP 6/7. Same model, different frame.
findings.proposed_fix: Model-review for NEW infrastructure must include incident history and ask which past incident each component prevents.

findings.summary: ~3h brainstorm + review + plan for 23 LOC output. 47 ideas, 12 explored, 7 planned, 1 built. 2% yield.
findings.evidence: Brainstorm synthesis + model-review + implementation plan artifacts vs final 23-line commit
findings.proposed_fix: Apply pain-point gate BEFORE model-review, not after. Brainstorm -> gate -> review survivors only.

findings.summary: Gemini CLI silently dropped -f context file. Produced hallucinated enterprise supply chain review instead of genomics pipeline review. No error signal.
findings.evidence: reprompt-gemini.md reviewed 'Layer 1: Commit Signing' — not in our context. Fixed with --stream.
findings.proposed_fix: Sanity-check model-review outputs: grep for 3 distinctive terms from context. If 0 matches, flag as context-drop.
